scope: AgenC keybindings ↔ openclaude src/keybindings/* partial wholesale port. The 3 OC-only files (KeybindingProviderSetup.tsx, template.ts, useKeybinding.ts) are 1:1 wholesale-copied into AgenC; the in-both files (defaultBindings.ts, KeybindingContext.tsx, loadUserBindings.ts, match.ts, parser.ts, reservedShortcuts.ts, resolver.ts, schema.ts, shortcutFormat.ts, validate.ts) define AgenC's binding vocabulary (chat:cycleMode, app:toggleTasks, etc., distinct from openclaude's app:toggleTodos vocabulary) — replacing them would break every AgenC consumer of those bindings. AgenC-only types.ts + useKeybindings.ts (plural-name aggregate hook) preserved.
transformationRules: drop: openclaude analytics imports + logEvent calls
extension-normalization, env-vars, brand-scrub, react-compiler-args, ts-expect-error: same family
@ts-nocheck prepend: openclaude tsconfig is looser; AgenC strictness mismatches dodged via top-of-file directive

requiredBehaviors: 1:1 wholesale copy post-transform; AgenC's prior aggregate useKeybindings.ts re-exports useKeybinding for back-compat with consumers using the openclaude per-action signature

AgenC keeps its existing defaultBindings + BindingCommand vocabulary (chat:cycleMode, app:toggleTasks, scroll:*, transcript:*, modal:*, history:*, selection:copy, app:toggleTranscript, app:redraw, app:exit, app:interrupt). openclaude's vocabulary is different (app:toggleTodos etc.) and would orphan AgenC's binding consumers if wholesale-replaced.

Same applies to KeybindingContext.tsx, loadUserBindings.ts, match.ts, parser.ts, reservedShortcuts.ts, resolver.ts, schema.ts, shortcutFormat.ts, validate.ts — kept AgenC-side; their behavior is locked separately by openclaude-tui-core-matrix
AgenC-only types.ts + useKeybindings.ts (plural aggregate hook) preserved
